Title: Reproductive Freedom as Democratic Architecture
Core Thesis (one sentence): Restrictions on reproductive autonomy function as civic and economic exclusion mechanisms — suppressing voter registration and turnout for the single largest constituency whose participation determines 2026 midterm outcomes.
Primary Constituency: Reproductive justice and women's rights organizations; ballot initiative campaign networks
Key Organizations: Center for Reproductive Rights; SisterSong; National Network of Abortion Funds; Reproductive Freedom for All; NARAL; Planned Parenthood Action Fund (51 affiliates)

Title: Criminal Justice Abolitionism and the Architecture of Democratic Exclusion
Core Thesis (one sentence): Mass incarceration is a systematic civic exclusion architecture — 4.4 million disenfranchised Americans; 1 in 13 Black adults unable to vote; jury exclusion; employment bars; housing discrimination — and the M4BL ecosystem is the highest-leverage distribution broker in Phase 2.
Primary Constituency: Abolitionist and criminal justice reform organizations; formerly incarcerated communities
Key Organizations: Critical Resistance; M4BL Policy Table; Brennan Center Justice Program; Worth Rises; Sentencing Project; Prison Policy Initiative

Title: Environmental Justice and Community Democratic Participation in Environmental Decision-Making
Core Thesis (one sentence): NEPA's elimination on January 8 2026 removed the primary mechanism through which low-income and minority communities could contest pollution-burden decisions — making environmental justice a democratic participation crisis not merely a regulatory policy question.
Primary Constituency: Environmental justice frontline communities; indigenous environmental organizations
Key Organizations: Climate Justice Alliance; WE ACT for Environmental Justice; Deep South Center for Environmental Justice; Earthjustice; Indigenous Environmental Network

Title: LGBTQ+ Rights and Trans Liberation Under Systematic Legal Attack
Core Thesis (one sentence): The 740 anti-LGBTQ+ bills in 42 states in 2026 share a common legal author — Alliance Defending Freedom — whose template infrastructure is identical to the architecture attacking reproductive rights (D36) and ballot initiatives (D33 Phase 1) making LGBTQ+ rights a structural convergence point with multiple Phase 1 threats.
Primary Constituency: LGBTQ+ and trans rights organizations; movement infrastructure connected to reproductive justice
Key Organizations: Advocates for Trans Equality; Lambda Legal; National LGBTQ Task Force; GLAAD; GLSEN

Title: Tenant Rights Housing Justice and Democratic Exclusion
Core Thesis (one sentence): Eviction is a voter suppression mechanism — Slee and Desmond (2023) establish a causal link between eviction rates and voter turnout suppression — and the OBBBA's rescission of the 30-day eviction notice rule in February 2026 is a direct attack on the democratic participation infrastructure of America's 44 million renter households.
Primary Constituency: Tenant organizing federations; homeless coalition staffers; housing court advocates
Key Organizations: National Tenant Union Federation; Right to the City Alliance; National Low Income Housing Coalition; National Housing Law Project; Legal Aid organizations

Title: Labor Rights NLRB Capture and Worker Power as Democratic Infrastructure
Core Thesis (one sentence): The Fifth Circuit's August 2025 ruling that the NLRB's structure is likely unconstitutional uses the same removal-power constitutional mechanism that destroyed independent regulatory authority across Phase 1 — making the labor rights crisis a democracy crisis with AFL-CIO's 12.5 million members as the framework's largest potential constituency.
Primary Constituency: Labor unions; worker center movement; economic justice organizations
Key Organizations: AFL-CIO Research Department; SEIU Legislative Affairs; CWA; Economic Policy Institute; Labor Notes

Title: Campaign Finance Dark Money and Corporate Capture of Democratic Institutions
Core Thesis (one sentence): Dark money at $1.9 billion in 2024 (a new record) is the structural mechanism connecting Citizens United to every sector-specific corporate capture documented in Phase 1 — crypto-PAC financial regulation; pharma lobbying; anti-union corporate campaigns; ballot initiative suppression — making this the meta-domain that names the connective tissue.
Primary Constituency: Campaign finance reform organizations; cross-partisan good-government networks
Key Organizations: Campaign Legal Center; Common Cause; Brennan Center; Issue One; End Citizens United

Title: Migrant and Refugee Rights Beyond Detention — Civic Participation and the Chilling Effect of Mass Enforcement
Core Thesis (one sentence): Mass deportation enforcement and community surveillance suppress the civic participation of both undocumented immigrants and their US citizen family members and neighbors — a democratic chilling effect that Phase 1's detention coverage does not address and that affects 12.2 million undocumented people plus 5.5 million mixed-status households.
Primary Constituency: Immigrant rights and legal services organizations; mixed-status community networks
Key Organizations: National Immigration Law Center; United We Dream; RAICES; Centro de los Derechos del Migrante; ACLU Immigrants' Rights Project

Title: Digital Justice Algorithmic Discrimination Broadband Equity and Platform Accountability
Core Thesis (one sentence): Private-sector algorithmic discrimination in hiring credit housing and insurance operates as structurally invisible civic exclusion — and the OBBBA's gutting of BEAD broadband funding (from $42.5B to $14B) removes the infrastructure prerequisite for participation in the digital civic space where democratic organizing increasingly occurs.
Primary Constituency: Digital rights organizations; civil rights organizations addressing algorithmic bias; rural broadband advocates
Key Organizations: Lawyers' Committee for Civil Rights Digital Justice Initiative; Color of Change; National Digital Inclusion Alliance; Electronic Frontier Foundation

Title: Youth Civic Power Student Organizing Voter Access and Civic Education Under Attack
Core Thesis (one sentence): Campus polling place closures; student ID restrictions; SAVE America Act barriers; OBBBA restrictions on Department of Education voter registration; and 6000+ student visa revocations with documented chilling effect constitute a coordinated structural attack on the voting cohort whose turnout volatility most affects competitive federal elections.
Primary Constituency: Student organizing networks; campus civic engagement organizations; youth civic power groups
Key Organizations: Campus Vote Project; Students Learn Students Vote; NextGen America; Young Invincibles; CIRCLE (Tufts University)

Title: Food Systems Agricultural Justice and Rural Democratic Organizing
Core Thesis (one sentence): Four corporations controlling 80% of beef processing; the House Farm Bill's $14B SNAP and conservation cuts; and two years of Farm Bill expiration constitute a democratic governance failure in the food system that reaches a rural constituency entirely absent from both Phase 1 and proposed Phase 2 contact lists.
Primary Constituency: Agricultural justice organizations; rural cooperatives; food sovereignty networks
Key Organizations: National Family Farm Coalition; National Black Food and Justice Alliance; Rural Coalition; Farm Aid; National Sustainable Agriculture Coalition

Title: Church-State Separation and Christian Nationalism as Institutional Project
Core Thesis (one sentence): Christian nationalism is not a cultural phenomenon but an organized legal infrastructure — the Alliance Defending Freedom model legislation pipeline; the Federalist Society judicial placement apparatus; and the SCOTUS abandonment of the Lemon test in Kennedy v. Bremerton — constituting a systematic project to transform Establishment Clause protections into preferences using the same infrastructure documented across Phase 1 judicial and state autocratization domains.
Primary Constituency: Secularist and religious freedom organizations; civil liberties organizations
Key Organizations: Americans United for Separation of Church and State; Freedom From Religion Foundation; Baptist Joint Committee; AU Action

Title: Pension Security Social Security and Retirement Justice as Democratic Infrastructure
Core Thesis (one sentence): SSA's elimination of 7000 jobs creates de facto benefit denials through processing delays; the OBBBA accelerates Trust Fund insolvency by three years; and public pension attacks in 14 states constitute an attack on the retirement security of 73.5 million Social Security recipients who are the most reliable voting cohort in American elections.
Primary Constituency: Senior organizations; public employee unions; retirement security advocates
Key Organizations: AARP (38M members); Economic Policy Institute; National Public Pension Coalition; Social Security Works; Alliance for Retired Americans

Title: Mental Health Psychiatric Survivor Rights and Community Crisis Response
Core Thesis (one sentence): The administration's push for expanded forced institutionalization; OBBBA gutting of the 988 Suicide and Crisis Lifeline; and jails as de facto psychiatric institutions represent a simultaneous attack on community-based care infrastructure and criminalization of mental health crisis — converging the healthcare abolitionist and disability rights domains with no Phase 1 coverage.
Primary Constituency: Mental health advocacy organizations; psychiatric survivor movement; disability rights organizations
Key Organizations: MindFreedom International; National Coalition for Mental Health Recovery; Disability Rights Advocates; National Alliance on Mental Illness

Title: Economic Democracy Worker Ownership Community Wealth and Participatory Budgeting
Core Thesis (one sentence): 3500+ worker cooperatives; 300+ community land trusts; and 60+ participatory budgeting jurisdictions constitute an existing democratic economy infrastructure that positions the framework as producing affirmative alternatives — the only domain on this list motivated by constructive vision rather than threat response and the entry point for constituencies that reject purely diagnostic framing.
Primary Constituency: Worker ownership and cooperative economy advocates; community wealth building organizations
Key Organizations: Democracy Collaborative; US Federation of Worker Cooperatives; New Economy Coalition; National Center for Employee Ownership; Participatory Budgeting Project
